title: Projects
text:
Human Performance in Operation of Small Modular Reactors
Team Lead, 2025
Directed research to inform the Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission’s emerging regulations for small modular reactors. Developed approaches for measuring human performance, integrating automation, managing multi-unit operations, and assessing community impacts.






User Experiments in Machine Learning Explainability
Researcher, 2024
Led controlled studies testing how different types of explanations affected users’ ability to detect ML errors. Showed that combining explanation types improved performance but also introduced risks, contributing to more responsible and human-centered AI design.





Trust in Human-ML Interaction
Working Group Member, 2024
Created a glyph-based polar chart to help engineers compare ML models and interpret results using explanation methods (LIME, SHAP, ELI5). User testing demonstrated the tool improved identification of key variables and supported review of model outputs.





Machine Learning Interface Design for Reliability Engineers
MITACS Accelerate, 2023
Created a glyph-based polar chart to help engineers compare ML models and interpret results using explanation methods (LIME, SHAP, ELI5). User testing demonstrated the tool improved identification of key variables and supported review of model outputs.

title: User Experiments in ML Explainability
text: **University of Toronto** PhD research; Developed methods to evaluate operator interaction with automated process control systems + human subjects experiments data.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Tools for Reliability Engineers
text: This project developed a glyph-based polar chart (GPC) to enhance the interpretability of machine learning models for data scientists. The tool enables comparisons of explanations across different models and computational methods. User experience evaluations with Ericsson data scientists showed that the GPC helped identify key model variables, compare various explanation techniques, and perform logical reviews of model outputs. This project was conducted during my internship at Ericsson’s Global AI Accelerator. The development of the prototype and its evaluation with Ericsson’s data scientists was published in the Journal of Ergonomics in Design and is available here.

title: Projects
text:
User Experiments in ML Explainability





Developed cognitive science–based methods to improve the explainability of machine learning models. Designed and conducted user experiments to evaluate user reliance on automated results. Findings showed that combining normative, contrastive, and counterfactual explanations can enhance user performance, while revealing potential limitations.















Human–Automation Interaction in Operation of Small Modular Reactors





Univeristy of Toronto Postdoctoral research; focused on operator decision-making and interface design for operation of small modular reactors. Funded by NSERC and CNSC. 





Jun 1, 2025












Explainable Machine Learning Tools for Reliability Engineers





Ericsson’s Gobal AI Accelerator, Mitacs Accelerate Internship. Design and evaluation of ML interfaces to support engineers’ decision-making and usability.
